verse_text: 'I found Israel as grapes in the wilderness, And I saw their fathers as an early guard at their fig tree. They went in to Baal Peor And were shamefully estranged; And the beloved ones became abominable.

pericope.title: Israel Will Return to the Lord

links.context: It was not the season for figs means that this fig tree had sprouted an early full foliage, indicating a first crop, but without bearing any fruit. Jesus, finding not even one fig, condemns it. In Scripture a fig tree is often a symbol of Israel ([[HOS.9:10]]). Her fruitfulness has ceased, so the Kingdom will be taken from her and given to another people, who are called to bear spiritual fruit (see [[MAT.21:43]]; [[GAL.5:22]], [[GAL.5:23]]; see note at Mt 21:19).

The fig tree is a figure of Israel, God's people before the Incarnation ([[HOS.9:10]]; Mt 21:19-21; [[MRK.11:13]], [[MRK.11:20]], [[MRK.11:21]]; [[LUK.13:6]], [[LUK.13:7]]). But the figs are young and immature, like those believers who have not grown in their faith ([[1CO.3:1]]). The true vine is Christ ([[JOH.15:1]]-5), and the vines are the Church, the New Israel. We are the fragrance of Christ to God the Father ([[2CO.2:15]]).
